headline: Blight check due tomorrow — 91°F heat by Friday
weather_notes: Water c2–c4 bean beds and potato_bags Thursday — 90°F Wednesday burns off today's irrigation from the shallow seedling zone by day 2 with zero rain in the 7-day forecast.
Water e1–e4 tomatoes deeply Thursday — 89–91°F heat builds through Friday with no rain; Thursday is the 2-day rewater window after today's Rachio run.
Hold manual watering on w2/w3 garlic now — heat wave is accelerating natural dry-down at 43 days to harvest; complete stop due June 21.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow; inspect lower leaves before 90°F Wednesday heat accelerates any latent infection into rapid spread.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: One day overdue with bed freshly irrigated today — ideal window to apply fish emulsion before 90°F heat reduces nutrient uptake.

suggested_tasks.title: Check potato_bags for frost recovery
suggested_tasks.description: Two sprouts frost-damaged May 23 (10 days ago); Wednesday's first 90°F day is the heat-stress test — confirm recovery before temps build to 91°F Friday.
